title: epitrachys lanceolata
intro: Epitrachys lanceolata, also known as Lance-leaved Epitrachys, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and indigestion, due to its antispasmodic and carminative effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits including relief from nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, as well as potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Epitrachys lanceolata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
